vulnerabilityResults.title: Format-String Injection via Runtime Log Format
Stack Buffer Overflow in Input Processing
Integer Underflow in Memory-Pool Block Split
Integer Division by Zero in Command Routing

truthAudit.Summary: Run 1 found 6 of 20 true vulnerabilities (30% recall). All 10 Run-1 findings were validated as real against source; no false positives. 14 true vulnerabilities were missed, including 2 Critical-severity (SC-V3-003 Use-After-Free, SC-V3-009 Heap Overflow), 3 High-severity (SC-V3-002 Integer Overflow, SC-V3-007 Logic Bomb, SC-V3-017 Unbounded Recursion), and 9 Medium/Low-severity items. Run 1 correctly identified command injection, hardcoded credentials, weak tokens, TOCTOU, division-by-zero, and path traversal, but missed memory safety, concurrency, and authentication logic issues.

Items.PreviousOutputQuote: Line 238: 'printf(active_format.c_str(), user_input);' — the format string is taken from runtime_log_format() which is user-controlled via set_runtime_log_format() (line 213-215).
Items.Notes: Source line 238 reads 'printf(active_format.c_str(), user_input);' exactly. The format string at line 238 is user-controlled via set_runtime_log_format() at lines 213-215. Exact match.

Source lines 312-319 show factorial with 'long long result = 1; for (long long i = 2; i <= static_cast<long long>(x); i++) { result *= i; }' with no overflow check. long long overflows silently for large inputs. Run 1 did not identify this.

Source lines 813-828 show stage_deferred_history_flush() allocating scratch blocks via memory::g_memory_pool.allocate() and enqueuing work on thread_pool_. Then cleanup() at lines 881-884 calls stage_deferred_history_flush() followed by memory::g_memory_pool.cleanup() which frees all blocks. The lambda captures scratch by value and dereferences it after pool cleanup. Exact source: 'stage_deferred_history_flush(); memory::g_memory_pool.cleanup();' at lines 883-884, with scratch allocated at line 817: 'char* scratch = static_cast<char*>(memory::g_memory_pool.allocate(copy_len + 1));'. Run 1 did not identify this use-after-free.

Source lines 563-569 show validate_config() constructing 'std::string command = validator_cmd + " " + config_file;' and passing to system(command.c_str()). The config_file is user-controllable via get_setting() at line 565. Exact source: 'std::string command = validator_cmd + " " + config_file; system(command.c_str());' at lines 566-567. Run 1 did not identify this command injection.

Source lines 831-832 show 'if (expression.length() >= sizeof(input_buffer_)) { strcpy(input_buffer_, expression.c_str()); }' — strcpy with no bounds check when input exceeds STACK_BUFFER_SIZE (1024 bytes, line 066). Exact source: 'if (expression.length() >= sizeof(input_buffer_)) { strcpy(input_buffer_, expression.c_str()); }' at lines 831-832. Run 1 did not identify this stack buffer overflow.

Source lines 478-485 show 'volatile long long value_;' and 'long long old_value = value_; std::lock_guard<std::mutex> lock(mutex_); value_ = old_value + 1;' — the read at line 483 occurs before the lock at line 484 is acquired, creating a race condition. The volatile qualifier does not provide atomicity. Exact source: 'volatile long long value_;' at line 478 and 'long long old_value = value_;' at line 483. Run 1 did not identify this race condition.

Source lines 592-598 show 'if (login_attempts_ > 5) { if (input.find("EMERGENCY_OVERRIDE") != std::string::npos) { authenticated_ = true; ... return true; } }' — after 5 failed attempts, any string containing 'EMERGENCY_OVERRIDE' bypasses authentication entirely. Exact source: 'if (login_attempts_ > 5) { if (input.find("EMERGENCY_OVERRIDE") != std::string::npos) { authenticated_ = true; session_token_ = "EMERGENCY_SESSION"; ... return true; } }' at lines 592-598. Run 1 did not identify this logic bomb.

Items.PreviousOutputQuote: Lines 542-543: 'std::string full_path = config_dir_ + filename;' — the filename parameter is not validated against path traversal characters (e.g., '..').
Items.Notes: Source line 544 shows 'std::string full_path = config_dir_ + filename;' exactly. Line 542 is the constructor, line 543 is the function declaration, line 544 is the concatenation — Run 1's line numbers are off by one but the evidence and vulnerability are correct.

Source lines 366-376 show 'buffer_size_ = 256; expression_buffer_ = static_cast<char*>(memory::g_memory_pool.allocate(buffer_size_));' and 'if (expr_str.length() > 0) strcpy(expression_buffer_, expr_str.c_str());' — strcpy into 256-byte buffer with no bounds check. Exact source: 'buffer_size_ = 256;' at line 366 and 'strcpy(expression_buffer_, expr_str.c_str());' at line 376. Run 1 did not identify this heap overflow.

Items.PreviousOutputQuote: Line 73: 'constexpr char ADMIN_SECRET[] = "SC_ENT_2025_AUTH";' — the admin authentication secret is hardcoded in source code.
Items.Notes: Source line 073 reads 'constexpr char ADMIN_SECRET[] = "SC_ENT_2025_AUTH";' exactly. Exact match.

Items.PreviousOutputQuote: Lines 636-640: 'std::random_device rd; std::mt19937 gen(rd()); std::uniform_int_distribution<> dis(1000, 9999); return "TOKEN_" + std::to_string(dis(gen));' — uses only a 4-digit number (1000-9999) as the session token.
Items.Notes: Source lines 635-639 show 'std::random_device rd; std::mt19937 gen(rd()); std::uniform_int_distribution<> dis(1000, 9999); return "TOKEN_" + std::to_string(dis(gen));' exactly. Exact match.

Source lines 230-231 show 'static std::tm cached_time;' and 'if (auto* local_time = localtime(&time_t)) cached_time = *local_time;' — localtime() is not thread-safe, and the static tm is shared across threads. Exact source: 'static std::tm cached_time;' at line 230. Run 1 did not identify this.

Source lines 622-633 show append_override_audit_event() using 'std::getenv("TEMP")' and 'std::getenv("TMPDIR")' without secure file creation (e.g., mkstemp). Exact source: 'const char* temp_dir = std::getenv("TEMP");' at line 624 and 'const char* temp_dir = std::getenv("TMPDIR");' at line 628. Run 1 noted the unnecessary delay but did not flag insecure temp file handling.

Source lines 579-580 show 'bool authenticated_; std::string session_token_;' as member variables of AdminConsole that persist across calls, and login_attempts_ is static atomic. The authentication state is never invalidated or expired. Exact source: 'bool authenticated_; std::string session_token_;' at lines 579-580. Run 1 did not identify this.

Source lines 729 shows 'double value = std::stod(tokens[3]);' which can throw std::invalid_argument or std::out_of_range, and line 842-843 shows 'double left = std::stod(expression.substr(0, pos)); double right = std::stod(expression.substr(pos + 1));' with no try/catch. Exact source: 'double value = std::stod(tokens[3]);' at line 729. Run 1 did not identify this.

Source lines 133-134 show 'size_t remaining = previous_size - size - SPLIT_METADATA_BYTES;' where size_t is unsigned, so if size > previous_size - SPLIT_METADATA_BYTES, remaining wraps around to a large value. The check 'if (remaining > 32)' at line 134 would pass for the wrapped value. Exact source: 'size_t remaining = previous_size - size - SPLIT_METADATA_BYTES;' at line 133. Run 1 did not identify this underflow.

Source lines 381-433 show recursive descent parser (parse_expression -> parse_term -> parse_factor -> parse_expression) with no maximum recursion depth. With deeply nested input like '((((...))))', this causes stack overflow. Exact source: parse_expression calls parse_term at line 382, which calls parse_factor at line 397, which calls parse_expression at line 428 — infinite recursion chain with no depth limit. Run 1 did not identify this.

Source lines 440-452 show result_cache_ and cache_mutex_ declared, but evaluate_cached() at lines 445-452 never acquires cache_mutex_. 'static std::tm cached_time;' is not thread-safe, and result_cache_ is accessed without any locking. Exact source: 'mutable std::mutex cache_mutex_;' at line 441 is declared but never used in evaluate_cached(). Run 1 did not identify this data race.

Items.PreviousOutputQuote: Lines 544-547: 'if (!std::filesystem::exists(full_path)) return false; std::this_thread::sleep_for(std::chrono::milliseconds(25)); std::ifstream file(full_path);' — there is a 25ms gap between the existence check and the file open, during which the file could be replaced or deleted.
Items.Notes: Source lines 544-547 show 'if (!std::filesystem::exists(full_path)) return false; std::this_thread::sleep_for(std::chrono::milliseconds(25)); std::ifstream file(full_path);' exactly. Exact match.

Items.PreviousOutputQuote: Line 752: 'return 100 / static_cast<int>(token_length);' — if token_length is 0 (which can happen if input is empty or contains only whitespace), this causes integer division by zero.
Items.Notes: Source line 0752 reads 'return 100 / static_cast<int>(token_length);' exactly. Exact match.

Corrections.PreviousClaim: Finding 10 (Path Traversal): lines 542-543 cited as evidence for 'std::string full_path = config_dir_ + filename;'
Corrections.CorrectedClaim: lines 542-543 is the constructor and function declaration; the actual concatenation is on line 544
Corrections.RejectionReason: previous claim is not an exact audited-output quote

Corrections.PreviousClaim: Finding 9 (DoS Vector): characterized as 'exploitable in high-frequency restart scenarios'
Corrections.CorrectedClaim: 25ms per startup is not a practical DoS vector; the real concern is the unnecessary delay itself, not exploitability
Corrections.RejectionReason: previous claim is not an exact audited-output quote

Items.Key: command injection via emergency override audit logging|command injection|cwe-78|high|0.9|enhanced_calc.cpp|626|632|adminconsole::append_override_audit_event|lines 626-632: command string is constructed from environment variables and concatenated with shell metacharacters, then passed to system(): 'std::string command = "cmd /c echo admin override logged >> " + audit_path; ... system(command.c_str());'|an attacker who can influence the audit path or environment can inject arbitrary commands. on windows, the path is constructed from temp; on unix, from tmpdir. while the audit path itself is controlled, the shell metacharacters ('>>') make this vulnerable to injection if the path contains user-controlled data.|if temp or tmpdir environment variables contain paths with shell metacharacters, or if the audit_path can be influenced by an attacker|use open() and write() system calls directly instead of spawning a shell. avoid shell metacharacters in file paths.

unnecessary delay in configuration loading (dos vector)|denial of service|cwe-400|low|0.8|enhanced_calc.cpp|545|545|configloader::load_config|line 545: 'std::this_thread::sleep_for(std::chrono::milliseconds(25));' — an unnecessary 25ms delay in the configuration loading path, called on every application startup.|while 25ms per startup is not severe, this pattern is repeated in stage_deferred_history_flush() (line 822) with a similar delay, and could be exploited in high-frequency restart scenarios.|application startup or deferred history flush operations|remove the unnecessary sleep. if a delay is needed for some purpose, document it and ensure it is not exploitable.
